repo.name: codex
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

prompt: Explain how `codex exec --json` flows from the top-level CLI into the exec runtime, app-server thread and turn start requests, and JSONL event output. Cite the source files that support the path.

expected_symbols.name: Subcommand::Exec
expected_symbols.why: Top-level CLI subcommand that routes non-interactive execution.

expected_symbols.name: codex_exec::Cli
expected_symbols.why: Defines exec-specific flags such as --json, --ephemeral, prompt, and resume/review subcommands.

expected_symbols.name: run_main
expected_symbols.why: Main exec runtime entry point after CLI parsing.

expected_symbols.name: run_exec_session
expected_symbols.why: Creates the event processor, starts the app-server client, and sends thread/turn requests.

expected_symbols.name: ThreadStartParams
expected_symbols.why: Carries model, cwd, permissions, sandbox, and thread metadata into the app-server thread start.

expected_symbols.name: TurnStartParams
expected_symbols.why: Carries the user prompt and turn settings into the app-server turn start.

expected_symbols.name: EventProcessor
expected_symbols.why: Abstracts human and JSON event output handling.

expected_symbols.name: EventProcessorWithJsonOutput
expected_symbols.why: Maps app-server notifications into JSONL thread events.

expected_claims.text: The top-level Codex CLI has an Exec subcommand that delegates non-interactive execution into the codex_exec crate.
The codex-exec binary parses exec-specific CLI options and calls codex_exec::run_main.
The exec CLI defines --json as the switch that chooses JSONL stdout output.
run_main loads config, resolves sandbox and approval settings, and builds the in-process app-server start arguments.
run_exec_session chooses EventProcessorWithJsonOutput in JSON mode and EventProcessorWithHumanOutput otherwise.
run_exec_session starts or resumes an app-server thread, then sends TurnStartParams with the prompt input.
EventProcessorWithJsonOutput serializes typed thread events as JSON lines on stdout.

forbidden_claims.text: codex exec bypasses the app-server path and talks directly to the model provider.
--json is handled by printing human output and converting it after the run completes.
ThreadStartParams and TurnStartParams are only used by the interactive TUI, not by codex exec.

notes: Local-real task for the user-requested sibling checkout. This is not public promotion evidence unless rerun against a pinned clean checkout with repeated paired rows.
